meaning: vt.<古>痛打,不断辱骂和嘲弄,就...作过分的冗长的讨论或分析等
examples: Don't worry, we won't belabor this topic.
We won't belabor the theory and background.
We would not belabor this point but only wish each to ponder it.
Do you belabor the description of your technology or its overwhelming multitude of features?
If he were a prizewinning political theorist, Krugman might raise an issue at once more serious and more grave than the one he chooses to belabor.
